{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Director of News",
  "description": "Talent City is a dynamic human capital firm specializing in talent sourcing, executive search, and strategic recruitment services across diverse industries. We connect high-performing professionals with forward-thinking organizations, helping businesses build agile, future-ready teams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Director of News

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos Mainland, Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and visionary Director of News to lead the editorial and operational direction of a 24-hour news station across Television, Digital, and Radio platforms.
	The ideal candidate will possess strong newsroom leadership experience, excellent editorial judgment, and a proven ability to manage fast-paced, multimedia news operations while driving audience growth and content excellence.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead and oversee newsroom operations across TV, Digital, and Radio platforms.
	Drive editorial strategy, news planning, and breaking news coverage.
	Ensure accurate, balanced, timely, and high-quality news output.
	Supervise Editors, Producers, Reporters, Anchors, and Digital teams.
	Develop innovative content and audience engagement strategies.
	Ensure compliance with media regulations, editorial policies, and journalism ethics.
	Manage newsroom resources, workflows, and operational efficiency.
	Collaborate with Programming, Production, Digital, and Engineering teams.
	Represent the station at industry events and stakeholder engagements.
	Mentor and develop newsroom talent and leadership capacity.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Mass Communication, Journalism, Media Studies, or related field.
	Minimum of 15 years of experience in broadcast journalism and newsroom operations across TV, Digital, and Radio.
	At least 5 years in a senior leadership role within a reputable media organization.
	Master&rsquo;s Degree or professional certifications will be an added advantage.
	Strong experience managing live news and 24-hour newsroom operations.
	Excellent understanding of digital journalism, newsroom technology, and audience engagement strategies.
	Strong knowledge of media laws, editorial ethics, and regulatory compliance.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-09",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-10",

Director of News Job at Talent City




        
    

        
        Talent City is a dynamic human capital firm specializing in talent sourcing, executive search, and strategic recruitment services across diverse industries. We connect high-performing professionals with forward-thinking organizations, helping businesses build agile, future-ready teams.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Director of News

Job Location: Lagos Mainland, Lagos

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced and visionary Director of News to lead the editorial and operational direction of a 24-hour news station across Television, Digital, and Radio platforms.
	The ideal candidate will possess strong newsroom leadership experience, excellent editorial judgment, and a proven ability to manage fast-paced, multimedia news operations while driving audience growth and content excellence.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead and oversee newsroom operations across TV, Digital, and Radio platforms.
	Drive editorial strategy, news planning, and breaking news coverage.
	Ensure accurate, balanced, timely, and high-quality news output.
	Supervise Editors, Producers, Reporters, Anchors, and Digital teams.
	Develop innovative content and audience engagement strategies.
	Ensure compliance with media regulations, editorial policies, and journalism ethics.
	Manage newsroom resources, workflows, and operational efficiency.
	Collaborate with Programming, Production, Digital, and Engineering teams.
	Represent the station at industry events and stakeholder engagements.
	Mentor and develop newsroom talent and leadership capacity.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Mass Communication, Journalism, Media Studies, or related field.
	Minimum of 15 years of experience in broadcast journalism and newsroom operations across TV, Digital, and Radio.
	At least 5 years in a senior leadership role within a reputable media organization.
	Master’s Degree or professional certifications will be an added advantage.
	Strong experience managing live news and 24-hour newsroom operations.
	Excellent understanding of digital journalism, newsroom technology, and audience engagement strategies.
	Strong knowledge of media laws, editorial ethics, and regulatory compliance.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Supply Chain Manager",
  "description": "Pullus is leveraging on data-driven technology to connect poultry farmers to affordable finance, better markets, advisory services, global best practices, and quality inputs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Supply Chain Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Kaduna
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Supply Chain Manager will ensure the seamless flow of inputs and products across Pullus&rsquo; farmer and customer network.
	This includes managing logistics, inventory, and distribution to guarantee efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and reliability. The role is critical to aligning production cycles with market demand, reducing wastage, and ensuring Pullus delivers at scale.


Key Responsibilities
Logistics &amp; Distribution:


	
	Supervise Logistics Officers to coordinate farm pickups, processing schedules, and customer deliveries.
	
	Negotiate and manage relationships with transport providers.
	Monitor cost per kg for distribution and optimize routes


Inventory Management:


	Supervise Inventory Officers to maintain accurate stock records.
	Reconcile incoming (farm collections), processing outputs, and customer dispatches.
	Ensure systems track availability in real time.


Supply &amp; Demand Alignment:


	Work with Sales to align customer orders with available stock.
	Coordinate with Extension/Procurement teams for timely input flow to farmers.


Technology &amp; Reporting:


	Use ERP/digital dashboards to track inventory, movement, and KPIs.
	Provide timely supply chain performance reports to management.


Risk &amp; Compliance:


	Identify and mitigate risks such as delays, shortages, or breakdowns in logistics.
	Ensure safety and regulatory compliance in transportation and storage handovers.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, or related field (Master&rsquo;s/MBA is an advantage).
	7&ndash;10 years of progressive experience in supply chain leadership, preferably in agribusiness or FMCG.
	Proven expertise in procurement, logistics, inventory, and financial models that support supply chain operations.
	Strong knowledge of cold chain management, distribution, and agricultural value chains.
	Excellent problem-solving, negotiation, and team leadership skills.
	Strong expertise in logistics and inventory systems (ERP/digital tools).


Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-20",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Pullus"
    
  },

Supply Chain Manager Job at Pullus




        
    

        
        Pullus is leveraging on data-driven technology to connect poultry farmers to affordable finance, better markets, advisory services, global best practices, and quality inputs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Supply Chain Manager

Job Location: Kaduna
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Supply Chain Manager will ensure the seamless flow of inputs and products across Pullus’ farmer and customer network.
	This includes managing logistics, inventory, and distribution to guarantee efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and reliability. The role is critical to aligning production cycles with market demand, reducing wastage, and ensuring Pullus delivers at scale.


Key Responsibilities
Logistics & Distribution:


	
	Supervise Logistics Officers to coordinate farm pickups, processing schedules, and customer deliveries.
	
	Negotiate and manage relationships with transport providers.
	Monitor cost per kg for distribution and optimize routes


Inventory Management:


	Supervise Inventory Officers to maintain accurate stock records.
	Reconcile incoming (farm collections), processing outputs, and customer dispatches.
	Ensure systems track availability in real time.


Supply & Demand Alignment:


	Work with Sales to align customer orders with available stock.
	Coordinate with Extension/Procurement teams for timely input flow to farmers.


Technology & Reporting:


	Use ERP/digital dashboards to track inventory, movement, and KPIs.
	Provide timely supply chain performance reports to management.


Risk & Compliance:


	Identify and mitigate risks such as delays, shortages, or breakdowns in logistics.
	Ensure safety and regulatory compliance in transportation and storage handovers.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Logistics, Business Administration, or related field (Master’s/MBA is an advantage).
	7–10 years of progressive experience in supply chain leadership, preferably in agribusiness or FMCG.
	Proven expertise in procurement, logistics, inventory, and financial models that support supply chain operations.
	Strong knowledge of cold chain management, distribution, and agricultural value chains.
	Excellent problem-solving, negotiation, and team leadership skills.
	Strong expertise in logistics and inventory systems (ERP/digital tools).


Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Executive (Fashion / Garment Production)",
  "description": "Bevany Resources is a recruitment and human talent support firm dedicated to connecting qualified candidates with forward-thinking employers. We specialise in talent acquisition, candidate screening, interview coordination, and providing recruitment support services to organisations across various industries. Our mission is to ensure businesses build strong teams while helping job seekers find meaningful career opportunities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive (Fashion / Garment Production)

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Fashion / Garment Production

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Business Development Executive to drive revenue growth, expand market presence within the fashion, and garment production space.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for identifying new business opportunities, securing corporate and bulk production deals (e.g., uniforms, workwear, and branded apparel), and building long-term client relationships.
	This role requires a strong understanding of the fashion production value chain, excellent sales skills, and the ability to convert leads into profitable business.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new business opportunities across corporate, institutional, and retail segments
	Generate leads and convert them into paying clients for bulk garment production (uniforms, corporate wear, event apparel, etc.)
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, vendors, and strategic partners
	Develop and execute business development strategies to meet revenue targets
	Prepare proposals, quotations, and negotiate contracts with clients
	Conduct market research to identify trends, customer needs, and competitor activities
	Collaborate with the production team to ensure client requirements are clearly communicated and delivered
	Track sales performance, pipeline, and conversion rates
	Represent the company at networking events, exhibitions, and client meetings
	Provide regular reports on business development activities and revenue performance


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an HND / Bachelor&#39;s Degree with 3&ndash;5 years of experience in Business Development, Sales, or Client Acquisition (preferably in fashion, manufacturing, or related industries)
	Proven track record of generating leads and closing deals
	Strong understanding of B2B sales, especially in bulk production or service-based environments
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills
	Ability to work independently and meet revenue targets
	Strong networking and relationship management skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and CRM tools


Key Competencies:


	Sales &amp; Negotiation
	Relationship Management
	Market Intelligence
	Strategic Thinking
	Result Orientation


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to:&nbsp;bevanyresources@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

        Bevany Resources is a recruitment and human talent support firm dedicated to connecting qualified candidates with forward-thinking employers. We specialise in talent acquisition, candidate screening, interview coordination, and providing recruitment support services to organisations across various industries. Our mission is to ensure businesses build strong teams while helping job seekers find meaningful career opportunities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive (Fashion / Garment Production)

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Fashion / Garment Production

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Business Development Executive to drive revenue growth, expand market presence within the fashion, and garment production space.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for identifying new business opportunities, securing corporate and bulk production deals (e.g., uniforms, workwear, and branded apparel), and building long-term client relationships.
	This role requires a strong understanding of the fashion production value chain, excellent sales skills, and the ability to convert leads into profitable business.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new business opportunities across corporate, institutional, and retail segments
	Generate leads and convert them into paying clients for bulk garment production (uniforms, corporate wear, event apparel, etc.)
	Build and maintain strong relationships with clients, vendors, and strategic partners
	Develop and execute business development strategies to meet revenue targets
	Prepare proposals, quotations, and negotiate contracts with clients
	Conduct market research to identify trends, customer needs, and competitor activities
	Collaborate with the production team to ensure client requirements are clearly communicated and delivered
	Track sales performance, pipeline, and conversion rates
	Represent the company at networking events, exhibitions, and client meetings
	Provide regular reports on business development activities and revenue performance


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an HND / Bachelor's Degree with 3–5 years of experience in Business Development, Sales, or Client Acquisition (preferably in fashion, manufacturing, or related industries)
	Proven track record of generating leads and closing deals
	Strong understanding of B2B sales, especially in bulk production or service-based environments
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills
	Ability to work independently and meet revenue targets
	Strong networking and relationship management skills

	Sales & Negotiation

	Market Intelligence
	Strategic Thinking

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Manager",
  "description": "Pullus is leveraging on data-driven technology to connect poultry farmers to affordable finance, better markets, advisory services, global best practices and quality inputs

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Kaduna&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Human Resources Manager will provide hands-on leadership for the full HR function in a fast-growing Agric-Tech startup. The role requires a pragmatic HR generalist who can build structures from the ground up while remaining agile and execution focused.
	The incumbent will support rapid scaling, talent acquisition, performance management, compliance, and culture-building across corporate, technology, and field operations.


Responsibilities
HR Strategy &amp; Organization Design:


	Develop and implement HR strategies aligned with the organization&rsquo;s growth stage and business objectives.
	Work closely with the CEO and COO to support organizational design, workforce planning, and scalability.
	Establish fit-for-purpose HR policies, frameworks, and processes suitable for a lean startup environment.


Talent Acquisition &amp; Onboarding:


	Manage end-to-end recruitment across technical, field, operations, and corporate roles.
	Design cost-effective recruitment strategies suitable for a startup budget.
	Ensure efficient onboarding and early-stage employee integration.


Performance &amp; Productivity Management:


	Design and manage simple, effective performance management systems linked to business outcomes.
	Support managers in setting KPIs and conducting performance reviews.
	Drive a results-oriented and accountability-driven culture.


Learning &amp; Capability Development:


	Identify capability gaps and implement practical learning solutions.
	Coordinate internal and external training initiatives focused on operational effectiveness and leadership.
	Support succession planning and internal talent development.


Compensation, Payroll &amp; HR Operations:


	Administer payroll, benefits, and employee records.
	Support the design of competitive yet sustainable compensation structures.
	Ensure accuracy, confidentiality, and efficiency in HR operations.


Employee Relations:


	Serve as the primary point of contact for employee relations and grievance management.
	Build and sustain a positive startup culture aligned with company values.
	Lead employee engagement initiatives across office and field teams.


Compliance &amp; Risk Management:


	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labour laws and statutory requirements.
	Manage disciplinary processes, exits, and documentation in line with best practices.
	Continuously review HR policies to reflect evolving business needs.


Administrative &amp; Office Oversight:


	Provide functional supervision to the Administrative Officer and Front Desk Officer.
	Oversee office administration, facilities coordination, and front-desk operations.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Social Sciences, or related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of post-NYSC HR experience, preferably in a startup, Agric-Tech, technology, or operations-driven environment.
	CIPM Professional Qualification (Associate or Full Member) &ndash; Mandatory.
	Proven experience managing the full HR value chain.


Key Competencies:


	Strong understanding of Nigerian labour laws and HR best practices.
	Hands-on, execution-focused mindset.
	Ability to build HR systems from scratch.
	Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills.
	High integrity, confidentiality, and sound judgment.


Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-01",

Human Resource Manager Job at Pullus




        
    

        
        Pullus is leveraging on data-driven technology to connect poultry farmers to affordable finance, better markets, advisory services, global best practices and quality inputs

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Manager
Job Location: Kaduna 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Human Resources Manager will provide hands-on leadership for the full HR function in a fast-growing Agric-Tech startup. The role requires a pragmatic HR generalist who can build structures from the ground up while remaining agile and execution focused.
	The incumbent will support rapid scaling, talent acquisition, performance management, compliance, and culture-building across corporate, technology, and field operations.


Responsibilities
HR Strategy & Organization Design:


	Develop and implement HR strategies aligned with the organization’s growth stage and business objectives.
	Work closely with the CEO and COO to support organizational design, workforce planning, and scalability.
	Establish fit-for-purpose HR policies, frameworks, and processes suitable for a lean startup environment.


Talent Acquisition & Onboarding:


	Manage end-to-end recruitment across technical, field, operations, and corporate roles.
	Design cost-effective recruitment strategies suitable for a startup budget.
	Ensure efficient onboarding and early-stage employee integration.


Performance & Productivity Management:


	Design and manage simple, effective performance management systems linked to business outcomes.
	Support managers in setting KPIs and conducting performance reviews.
	Drive a results-oriented and accountability-driven culture.


Learning & Capability Development:


	Identify capability gaps and implement practical learning solutions.
	Coordinate internal and external training initiatives focused on operational effectiveness and leadership.
	Support succession planning and internal talent development.


Compensation, Payroll & HR Operations:


	Administer payroll, benefits, and employee records.
	Support the design of competitive yet sustainable compensation structures.
	Ensure accuracy, confidentiality, and efficiency in HR operations.


Employee Relations:


	Serve as the primary point of contact for employee relations and grievance management.
	Build and sustain a positive startup culture aligned with company values.
	Lead employee engagement initiatives across office and field teams.


Compliance & Risk Management:


	Ensure compliance with Nigerian labour laws and statutory requirements.
	Manage disciplinary processes, exits, and documentation in line with best practices.
	Continuously review HR policies to reflect evolving business needs.


Administrative & Office Oversight:


	Provide functional supervision to the Administrative Officer and Front Desk Officer.
	Oversee office administration, facilities coordination, and front-desk operations.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Social Sciences, or related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of post-NYSC HR experience, preferably in a startup, Agric-Tech, technology, or operations-driven environment.
	CIPM Professional Qualification (Associate or Full Member) – Mandatory.
	Proven experience managing the full HR value chain.


Key Competencies:


	Strong understanding of Nigerian labour laws and HR best practices.
	Hands-on, execution-focused mindset.
	Ability to build HR systems from scratch.
	Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills.
	High integrity, confidentiality, and sound judgment.


Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "title": "Quality Assurance Officer",
  "description": "Pullus is leveraging on data-driven technology to connect poultry farmers to affordable finance, better markets, advisory services, global best practices and quality inputs

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Assurance Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	As the Quality Assurance Officer at Pullus Africa, your primary responsibility will be to ensure the consistent production of high-quality, safe poultry products.
	This role will oversee all quality control measures throughout the poultry production process, from converting the raw materials to final product packaging and distribution.
	The Quality Assurance officer will work closely with the purchase, extension, and logistics teams to uphold rigorous quality standards and drive continuous improvement.


Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and maintain comprehensive quality assurance programs, including quality control procedures, inspections, and audits.
	Monitor production processes to identify and resolve quality issues in a timely manner.
	Conduct regular testing and analysis of raw materials, work-in-process, and finished poultry products to verify compliance with food safety regulations and internal quality specifications.
	Document quality control activities and maintain detailed records to demonstrate process control.
	Carry out research and development exercises to evaluate new technologies, equipment, and processes that can enhance product quality and operational efficiency.
	Train and supervise staff to ensure adherence to standard operating procedures.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for all quality-related inquiries from internal and external stakeholders.
	Stay up-to-date with relevant industry regulations, standards, and best practices in poultry production.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in food science, food technology, or a related field.
	3 - 5 years of experience in a quality assurance or quality control role within the poultry or food processing industry.
	Thorough understanding of HACCP, GMP, ISO and food safety standards.
	Proficiency in data analysis and the use of quality control software.
	Excellent documentation, auditing and reporting skills.
	Resides in Karu or its environs.

Quality Assurance Officer Job at Pullus




        
    

        
        Pullus is leveraging on data-driven technology to connect poultry farmers to affordable finance, better markets, advisory services, global best practices and quality inputs

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Assurance Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	As the Quality Assurance Officer at Pullus Africa, your primary responsibility will be to ensure the consistent production of high-quality, safe poultry products.
	This role will oversee all quality control measures throughout the poultry production process, from converting the raw materials to final product packaging and distribution.
	The Quality Assurance officer will work closely with the purchase, extension, and logistics teams to uphold rigorous quality standards and drive continuous improvement.


Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and maintain comprehensive quality assurance programs, including quality control procedures, inspections, and audits.
	Monitor production processes to identify and resolve quality issues in a timely manner.
	Conduct regular testing and analysis of raw materials, work-in-process, and finished poultry products to verify compliance with food safety regulations and internal quality specifications.
	Document quality control activities and maintain detailed records to demonstrate process control.
	Carry out research and development exercises to evaluate new technologies, equipment, and processes that can enhance product quality and operational efficiency.
	Train and supervise staff to ensure adherence to standard operating procedures.
	Serve as the primary point of contact for all quality-related inquiries from internal and external stakeholders.
	Stay up-to-date with relevant industry regulations, standards, and best practices in poultry production.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree in food science, food technology, or a related field.
	3 - 5 years of experience in a quality assurance or quality control role within the poultry or food processing industry.
	Thorough understanding of HACCP, GMP, ISO and food safety standards.
	Proficiency in data analysis and the use of quality control software.
	Excellent documentation, auditing and reporting skills.
	Resides in Karu or its environs.
